items.subtopic: Introduction to Biology lab component grading

format_metadata.title: BIO 101: Introduction to Biology — Laboratory Component

items.text:
BIO 101: Introduction to Biology — Laboratory Component
Instructor: Dr. Helena Park | Room: Science Hall 240

The laboratory portion accounts for 30% of your final course grade. It is divided as follows: weekly lab reports (15%), two practical exams (10%), and lab attendance and participation (5%).

Lab reports are due every Tuesday by 5:00 PM through the course portal. Reports submitted after the deadline lose 10% of their value per day, up to three days; after that, they receive no credit.

Attendance is mandatory. Students who miss more than two sessions without a documented excuse will be unable to pass the lab component. Each student must also purchase a lab safety kit, available at the campus store for $25.00, before the first session on September 9.

Make-up labs are offered only during the final week of the semester and must be arranged with Dr. Park at least one week in advance. Please review the full safety guidelines posted on the portal.

questions.stem: According to the syllabus, what portion of the final grade comes from weekly lab reports?
questions.explanation: The syllabus states that the lab portion is 30% overall, divided into weekly lab reports (15%), two practical exams (10%), and attendance and participation (5%). Reports are therefore worth fifteen percent. The other figures are real numbers from the breakdown attached to the wrong component.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a lab report submitted four days after the deadline?

options.A: It will lose ten percent of its value
options.B: It will be accepted with a documented excuse
options.D: It will be graded during the final week

questions.explanation: The syllabus says late reports lose 10% per day for up to three days, and after that they receive no credit. A report four days late is past that three-day window, so it would receive no credit. The other options reuse passage terms (10%, documented excuse, final week) but apply them to the wrong rule.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this syllabus excerpt?

options.A: To advertise a lab safety kit sold at the campus store
options.B: To explain how the laboratory component is graded and run
options.C: To announce a change in the course meeting room
options.D: To list the dates of the final practical exams

questions.explanation: The excerpt lays out the grade breakdown, deadline and late penalties, attendance rules, the required safety kit, and make-up policy — an overview of how the lab component is graded and run. The safety kit and exams are single details, and no room change is announced.
